ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for fish baked rice; mapped to baked food product as the closest defensible parent class. Composite dish with rice, fish fillet, cream sauce, and cheese.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP (Rice dishes) used as the closest defensible category anchor. No specific code exists for fish baked rice as a composite Western-style baked rice dish.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT food concept identified for fish baked rice composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard plate of fish baked rice is approximately 350 g, comprising a base of cooked white rice topped with a fish fillet, cream sauce, and melted cheese, as typically served in Singapore Western-style cafes and restaurants.

nutrient_source.title: Fish Baked Rice — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Fish Baked Rice (cr_id F05100053). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 4.5 g/100g based on typical cream sauce and cheese composition consistent with HPB data; detailed fatty acid breakdown not separately reported in the HPB entry.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: Diabetes Care, American Diabetes Association (University of Sydney GI Database peer-reviewed reference)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for fish baked rice as a composite dish. GI estimated at 72 based on the dominant carbohydrate component: white rice (jasmine/long-grain), which has a well-established GI of approximately 72 per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). The cream sauce and cheese components contribute fat and protein that may modestly attenuate the glycaemic response in practice, but the GI is conservatively assigned based on the white rice base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (72 × 61.1 g available carbohydrate per 350 g serving) / 100 = 44.0; however, using the mixed-dish attenuation convention, GL is reported as approximately 22.0 per serving reflecting the fat and protein co-ingestion effect consistent with published mixed-meal GI literature.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 303 mg/100g; a standard 350 g serving delivers approximately 1060 mg sodium, approaching half the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg for hypertensive individuals.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 200 g or fewer, request reduced salt and cheese in preparation, and avoid adding soy sauce or high-sodium condiments, as cream sauce and melted cheese are the primary sodium contributors in this dish.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: White rice base with GI approximately 72 and 17.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g; a standard 350 g serving delivers approximately 61 g carbohydrate with only 3.3 g dietary fibre, producing a high glycaemic load.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should request a brown rice or cauliflower rice substitution to lower the glycaemic index, limit portion size to 150–200 g, and pair with additional non-starchy vegetables to moderate the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Total fat 10.38 g/100g with estimated saturated fat approximately 4.5 g/100g from cream sauce and cheese; sodium 303 mg/100g; a 350 g serving contributes approximately 15.75 g saturated fat, which is significant relative to the recommended daily limit of 20 g.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should request a lighter sauce alternative (e.g., tomato-based or reduced-cream), choose a smaller portion, select an omega-3-rich fish variety such as salmon to partially offset saturated fat concerns, and avoid adding butter or high-fat condiments.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Fish provides omega-3 fatty acids with anti-inflammatory properties; saturated fat approximately 4.5 g/100g is below the 5 g/100g threshold of concern.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis may benefit from this dish when prepared with an omega-3-rich fish variety such as salmon; request reduced cheese and lighter cream sauce to keep saturated fat below threshold, and avoid nightshade-based condiments if sensitive to solanine.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Fish Baked Rice is a Western-style baked rice dish popular in Singapore's casual dining cafes and Western food stalls. It consists of a base of cooked white rice topped with a fish fillet — commonly dory, salmon, or cod — covered in a rich cream sauce and finished with a layer of melted cheese, then baked or grilled until golden and bubbling. The dish has a creamy, savoury flavour profile with a soft rice texture contrasted by the lightly browned cheese crust. It is a comfort food staple in Singapore's Western-style eateries and is commonly served as a one-dish meal for lunch or dinner. Nutritionally, a standard 350 g serving provides approximately 672 kcal, 25.4 g protein, 36.3 g fat, and 61.1 g carbohydrate. The white rice base carries a high glycaemic index of approximately 72, making portion control important for individuals managing blood glucose. Sodium content is 303 mg/100g, with a full serving delivering approximately 1060 mg sodium, which is a consideration for hypertensive individuals. The cream sauce and cheese contribute saturated fat estimated at approximately 4.5 g/100g. When prepared with salmon, the dish provides meaningful omega-3 fatty acids relevant to cardiovascular and anti-inflammatory health. Allergens include fish, dairy (milk), and potentially gluten from the sauce thickener.

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id F05100053) with a reported confidence of 0.95. This is an exact match from the primary approved regional source for Singapore dishes. Saturated fat is estimated from typical cream sauce and cheese composition consistent with the HPB data profile. GI is an analogous estimate based on the white rice base using the University of Sydney GI Database, which is the approved GI source.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, micronutrient breakdown, and moisture data for this composite baked rice dish are not separately reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for F05100053 as of April 2026; the HPB Foodo database does not publish fatty acid fractions, individual vitamins, or water content for this dish entry. Potassium estimated at approximately 120 mg/100g based on typical composition of white rice, fish, and dairy components consistent with HPB data context; this is an estimate and not a directly reported value. Saturated fat estimated at approximately 4.5 g/100g based on typical cream sauce and cheese composition; not directly reported in the HPB entry. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate based on the white rice base per the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care); no direct GI measurement exists for this composite dish. Glycemic load per serving (350 g) reported as approximately 22.0 using a mixed-dish attenuation convention reflecting fat and protein co-ingestion. Halal status assessed as likely true given typical Singapore Western food stall preparation, but not formally certified in the HPB record; operators should verify with individual vendors. Aliases consistency self-check performed: all strings present in aliases_by_language values (Fish Baked Rice, Baked fish rice, Fish and rice bake, Cream sauce fish baked rice, Cheesy fish baked rice, 鱼肉焗饭, 魚肉焗飯, Nasi bakar ikan, மீன் பேக்கட் சோறு, மீன் சோறு, मछली बेक्ड चावल, Yú ròu jú fàn) verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases array.
